name: DatetimeAdapter
description:
Datetime Adapter is an abstract class that must be implemented by each adapter.
It's used to encapsulate a date/time manipulations as result
others places that uses it remain date type agnostic.

methods.name: addCalendarDays
methods.description:
Adds the given number of days to the date. Days are counted as if moving one cell on the
calendar for each day.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date to add days to.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: The number of days to add (may be negative).

methods.name: addCalendarMonths
methods.description:
Adds the given number of months to the date. Months are counted as if flipping a page on the
calendar for each month and then finding the closest date in the new month. For example when
adding 1 month to Jan 31, 2017, the resulting date will be Feb 28, 2017.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date to add months to.

parameters.name: months
parameters.description: The number of months to add (may be negative).

methods.name: addCalendarYears
methods.description:
Adds the given number of years to the date. Years are counted as if flipping 12 pages on the
calendar for each year and then finding the closest date in the new month. For example when
adding 1 year to Feb 29, 2016, the resulting date will be Feb 28, 2017.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date to add years to.

parameters.name: years
parameters.description: The number of years to add (may be negative).

methods.name: createDate
methods.signature: (year: number, month?: number, date?: number) => D
methods.description: Creates a date with the given year, month, and date.

parameters.name: year
parameters.description: The full year of the date. (e.g. 1989).

parameters.name: month
parameters.description: The month of the date (1 = January). Must be an integer 1 - 12.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The month date. Must be an integer 1 - length of the given month.

Method that check equality of 2 dates.
Does not take into account time value

parameters.name: date1
parameters.description: The first date to compare with

parameters.name: date2

methods.name: toIso8601
methods.description:
Gets the ISO 8601 date-time string for the given date.

Returns a **local** (timezone-unaware) string in the format `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss`.
This is intentionally **not** RFC 3339 compliant because no timezone designator is appended.

This method is used to generate date strings that are compatible with native HTML attributes
such as the `min` or `max` attribute of an `<input>`.

To produce an RFC 3339 string, append a timezone designator to the result:
```ts
const rfc3339Utc = adapter.toIso8601(date) + 'Z';          // UTC
const rfc3339Local = adapter.toIso8601(date) + '+05:30';    // with offset
```

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date to get the ISO date string for.

methods.name: today
methods.description: Gets today's date where time value is set to 0
